commit.message:
Frontend: MBO as source of truth, compute MBP on render

Replace incremental MBP maintenance (addToLevel/removeFromLevel) with
pure MBO storage. The orders Map is the single source of truth.
renderBook() computes price levels on-the-fly by aggregating from
the orders map each frame.

This eliminates drift caused by conflation dropping intermediate
updates (e.g. Update+Delete conflated to Delete — client removed
from stale price level). With MBO, Add/Update is a simple map.set,
Delete is map.delete — always correct regardless of conflation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

1. Throughput.tsx page was calling useThroughput() without granularity,
   so the selector had no effect on the standalone Throughput page.

commit.message:
fix: validate schema version in build-pages before using cache/fixture data

When build-pages fell back to the committed fixture file (e.g. on a
push-triggered CI build before today's scheduled collection ran), it
silently used stale schema-v1 data that lacks mergedPRTimeline. This
caused allPRDetails to fall back to the 10-item pullRequestDetails list,
capping the Top Repos chart at 10 PRs per repo.

Now build-pages validates the schema version of both the daily cache file
and the fixture file and exits with a clear error if either is outdated,
rather than deploying a dashboard with incomplete data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace export pipeline with direct composited-frame streaming

Rewrites VideoEncoder to mirror the editor preview pipeline: source
JPEG frames are loaded via VideoFrameReader, composited by
FrameCompositor, and fed directly to a MediaStreamSource that streams
raw BGRA8 pixels to the MediaTranscoder for H.264 encoding.

This eliminates all intermediate temp files (the PNG/JPEG roundtrip
that was causing corrupt MP4 output with error 0xC00D36C4) and removes
the per-frame file I/O bottleneck entirely.

Audio is handled in a fast second pass: the video-only MP4 is combined
with source audio tracks via MediaComposition.

commit.message:
fix(ci): disable elevation requests when GH graph built without elevation

In CI the GraphHopper graph is built using config-ci.yml which sets
graph.elevation.provider: none (skips SRTM tile downloads for faster
graph builds). Requesting elevation=true against a non-elevation graph
causes GH to return HTTP 400 'Elevation not supported!', which fails
all routing tests.

AppHost now sets GRAPHHOPPER__ELEVATIONENABLED=false on the API when
GRAPHHOPPER_PREBUILT_IMAGE is set (i.e. CI mode). Program.cs reads this
setting and passes it through to BuildRouteRequest so all route and
export endpoints respect it.
